description: In this episode of the Delphi Podcast, John Fletcher discusses the Innovation Game (TIG), a decentralized science project aimed at accelerating algorithmic innovation. He explains how TIG uses a coordination protocol to improve algorithms for scientific problems through a proof of work system similar to Bitcoin. The conversation covers the project's origins, the challenges faced by scientists in securing funding, and the unique structure of open-source projects in the context of algorithm development.
topic_summary: John Fletcher introduces the Innovation Game (TIG), detailing its purpose to enhance algorithmic innovation through a decentralized approach. He highlights the project's unique proof of work mechanism and its market-driven evaluation of algorithms, while also reflecting on the challenges faced by researchers in obtaining funding for scientific endeavors [t=1:28 - t=3:60].
